content.text:
Sometimes when two materials mix together, something brand new is created! This is called a chemical reaction.

A chemical reaction is different from just mixing — the materials actually change into something new. You cannot easily undo it.

🌋 Baking Soda + Vinegar — Mix these together and you get a big fizz! Bubbles of carbon dioxide gas are created. This is a chemical reaction.

🍞 Baking Bread — When bread bakes in the oven, the dough changes and becomes fluffy bread. You cannot turn the bread back into dough!

🍎 A Cut Apple Turning Brown — When you cut an apple and leave it out, it slowly turns brown. That is a slow chemical reaction with the air.

🕯️ A Burning Candle — The wax burns and turns into light, heat, and gases. This is also a chemical reaction.

How do you know a chemical reaction happened? Look for these clues:
✅ Bubbles or fizzing
✅ Color change
✅ Heat or light produced
✅ A new smell
✅ Cannot be easily reversed

Not all mixing is a reaction! Stirring sugar into water is just dissolving — no new material is created. But mixing baking soda and vinegar creates a brand new gas!

diagrams.alt: Diagram showing baking soda and vinegar reaction with bubbles

experiment.instructions: Drag the baking soda into the vinegar and watch the chemical reaction!

combinations.resultLabel: A chemical reaction! The baking soda and vinegar create carbon dioxide gas — that is what makes all those bubbles and fizzing!

options.text: When you stir two liquids together gently
When materials change and create something new

practiceQuestions.hint: Think about what happened when baking soda and vinegar mixed — something new was created!

practiceQuestions.prompt: Which of these is a sign that a chemical reaction happened?

options.text: The material stays the same color

practiceQuestions.hint: Remember the baking soda and vinegar experiment — what did you see?

practiceQuestions.prompt: When baking soda and vinegar are mixed, what gas is produced?
practiceQuestions.hint: This is the same gas that makes soda drinks fizzy!

questions.prompt: Which of these is an example of a chemical reaction?

options.text: Baking soda and vinegar fizzing together

questions.prompt: How is a chemical reaction different from just mixing?

options.text: A chemical reaction is always cold
A chemical reaction creates a new material

questions.prompt: A cut apple turning brown is an example of what?

options.text: A slow chemical reaction with air

questions.prompt: Which of these is NOT a sign of a chemical reaction?
Can a chemical reaction usually be undone?

options.text: No, it is usually very hard or impossible to undo
Only if you cool it down
